title: Decimals in written form
description: Practice converting decimals between standard form and written form.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write nine and three hundred twenty-one thousandths in decimal notation.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The number is $\goldE{\text{nine}}$ and $\tealE{\text{three hundred}}$ $\purpleD{\text{twenty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{one thousandths}}$.

The last place value in the number is *thousandths*, so the number ends in the *thousandths* place.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$?$ | $?$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$ | $?$

The $\text{and}$ tells us where to put the decimal.

$\goldE{\text{Nine}}.\tealE{\text{three hundred}}$ $\purpleD{\text{twenty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{one thousandths}}$.

We write $\text{\goldE{nine}}$ to the left side of the decimal as $\goldE9$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$?$ | $\goldE{9}$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$ | $?$

We write $\text{\tealE{three hundred}}$ $\text{\purpleD{twenty-}}\text{\maroonD{one}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}}$ to the right side of the decimal as $\tealE3\purpleD{2}\maroonD{1}$ .

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $\goldE{9}$ |$.$  | $\tealE3$ | $\purpleD{2}$ | $\maroonD{1}$
The number $\goldE{\text{nine}}$ and $\tealE{\text{three hundred}}$ $\purpleD{\text{twenty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{one thousandths}}$ in decimal notation is $\goldE{9}.\tealE3\purpleD{2}\maroonD{1}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write two hundred six and four tenths in decimal notation.**

[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
The number is $\tealE{\text{two hundred}}$ $\goldE{\text{six}}$ and $\maroonE{\text{four tenths}}$.
The last place value in the number is *tenths*, so the number ends in the *tenths* place.

Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$?$ | $?$ |$?$  | $.$ | $?$

The $\text{and}$ tells us where to put the decimal.

$\tealE{\text{Two hundred}}\goldE{\text{ six}}.\maroonE{\text{four tenths}}$

We write $\tealE{\text{two hundred}}\goldE{\text{ six}}$ to the left side of the decimal as $\tealE20\goldE6$.

Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\tealE2$ | $0$ | $\goldE{6}$ |$.$  | $?$

We write $\maroonE{\text{four tenths}}$ to the right side of the decimal  as $\maroonE{4}$ .

Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\tealE2$ | $0$ | $\goldE{6}$ |$.$  | $\maroonE{4}$
The number $\tealE{\text{two hundred}}$ $\goldE{\text{six}}$ and $\maroonE{\text{four tenths}}$ in decimal notation is $\tealE20\goldE{6}.\maroonE{4}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write six and eight hundred seventy-three thousandths in decimal notation.**

[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
The number is $\goldE{\text{six}}$ and $\tealE{\text{eight hundred}}$ $\purpleD{\text{seventy}}$-$\maroonD{\text{three thousandths}}$.

The last place value in the number is *thousandths*, so the number ends in the *thousandths* place.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$?$|  $?$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$ | $?$

The $\text{and}$ tells us where to put the decimal.

$\goldE{\text{Six}}.\tealE{\text{eight hundred}}$ $\purpleD{\text{seventy}}$-$\maroonD{\text{three thousandths}}$.

We write $\text{\goldE{six}}$ to the left side of the decimal as $\goldE6$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$?$ | $\goldE{6}$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$ | $?$

We write $\tealE{\text{eight hundred}}$ $\purpleD{\text{seventy}}$-$\maroonD{\text{three thousandths}}$ to the right side of the decimal as $\tealE8\purpleD{7}\maroonD{3}$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $\goldE{6}$ |$.$  | $\tealE8$ | $\purpleD{7}$ | $\maroonD{3}$
The number $\goldE{\text{six}}$ and $\tealE{\text{eight hundred}}$ $\purpleD{\text{seventy}}$-$\maroonD{\text{three thousandths}}$ in decimal notation is $\goldE{6}.\tealE8\purpleD{7}\maroonD{3}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write two and forty-two thousandths in decimal notation.**

[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
The number is $\goldE{\text{two}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{forty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{two thousandths}}$.

The last place value in the number is *thousandths*, so the number ends in the *thousandths* place.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$?$ | $?$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$ | $?$

The $\text{and}$ tells us where to put the decimal.

$\goldE{\text{Two}}.\purpleD{\text{forty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{two thousandths}}$.

We write $\text{\goldE{two}}$ to the left side of the decimal as $\goldE2$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$?$ | $\goldE{2}$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$ | $?$

We write $\purpleD{\text{forty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{two thousandths}}$ to the right side of the decimal as $\purpleD{4}\maroonD{2}$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $\goldE{2}$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\purpleD{4}$ | $\maroonD{2}$
The number $\goldE{\text{two}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{forty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{two thousandths}}$ in decimal notation is $\goldE{2}.0\purpleD{4}\maroonD{2}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write one hundred twenty-one and twenty-one thousandths in decimal notation.**

[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
The number is $\redE{\text{one hundred}}\goldE{\text{ twenty-}}\tealE{\text{one}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{twenty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{one thousandths}}$.

The last place value in the number is *thousandths*, so the number ends in the *thousandths* place.

Hundreds| Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:| :-:
$?$| $?$ | $?$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$ | $?$

The $\text{and}$ tells us where to put the decimal.

$\redE{\text{One hundred}}\goldE{\text{ twenty-}}\tealE{\text{one}}.$$\purpleD{\text{twenty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{one thousandths}}$

We write $\redE{\text{one hundred}}\goldE{\text{ twenty-}}\tealE{\text{one}}$ to the left side of the decimal as $\redE1\goldE2\tealE1$.

Hundreds| Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\redE1$| $\goldE2$ | $\tealE1$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$ | $?$

We write $\purpleD{\text{twenty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{one thousandths}}$ to the right side of the decimal as $\purpleD{2}\maroonD{1}$ .

Hundreds| Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\redE1$| $\goldE2$ | $\tealE1$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\purpleD2$ | $\maroonD1$

The number $\redE{\text{one hundred}}\goldE{\text{ twenty-}}\tealE{\text{one}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{twenty}}$-$\maroonD{\text{one thousandths}}$ in decimal notation is $\redE1\goldE2\tealE1.0\purpleD{2}\maroonD{1}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write eighty and fifteen hundredths in decimal notation.**

[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
The number is $\goldE{\text{eighty}}$ and $\tealE{\text{fifteen hundredths}}$.

The last place value in the number is *hundredths*, so the number ends in the *hundredths* place.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$?$ | $?$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$

The $\text{and}$ tells us where to put the decimal.

$\goldE{\text{Eighty}}.\tealE{\text{fifteen hundredths}}$

We write $\goldE{\text{eighty}}$ to the left side of the decimal as $\goldE{80}$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldE8$ | $\goldE{0}$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$

We write $\tealE{\text{fifteen hundredths}}$ to the right side of the decimal as $\tealE{15}$ .

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldE8$| $\goldE{0}$ |$.$  | $\tealE1$ | $\tealE5$
The number $\goldE{\text{eighty}}$ and $\tealE{\text{fifteen hundredths}}$ in decimal notation is $\goldE{80}.\tealE{15}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write five hundred seven and seven hundredths in decimal notation.**

[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
The number is $\goldE{\text{five hundred}}\tealE{\text{ seven}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{seven hundredths}}$.

The last place value in the number is *hundredths*, so the number ends in the *hundredths* place.

Hundreds| Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths|
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:| :-:
$?$| $?$ | $?$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$

The $\text{and}$ tells us where to put the decimal.

$\goldE{\text{Five hundred}}\tealE{\text{ seven}}.$$\purpleD{\text{seven hundredths}}$.

We write $\goldE{\text{five hundred}}\tealE{\text{ seven}}$ to the left side of the decimal as $\goldE50\tealE7$.

Hundreds| Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths|
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldE5$| $0$ | $\tealE7$ |$.$  | $?$ | $?$

We write $\purpleD{\text{seven hundredths}}$ to the right side of the decimal as $\purpleD{7}$.

Hundreds| Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths|
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldE5$| $0$ | $\tealE7$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\purpleD7$
The number $\goldE{\text{five hundred}}\tealE{\text{ seven}}$ and $\purpleD{\text{seven hundredths}}$ in decimal notation is $\goldE50\tealE7.0\purpleD{7}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is $5.071$ in words?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $5.071$.

The last place value in the number is *thousandths*, so the number ends in the *thousandths* place.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $\goldD{5}$ |$.$  | $\greenD0$ | $\purpleC{7}$ | $\maroonD{1}$

The number $\goldD{5}$ is to the left of the decimal, so we can write it as $\text{\goldD{five}}$.

$\text{\goldD{Five}}-$

We can represent the decimal with the word $\blueD{\text{and}}$.

$\text{\goldD{Five}\blueD{ and}}-$

$.0\purpleC{7}\maroonD{1}$ is to the right of the decimal and ends in the *thousandths* place.

$\text{\purpleC{Seventy-}}\text{\maroonD{one}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}}$
We write $5.071$ in words as $\text{\goldD{five}{ and}}\text{\purpleC{ seventy-}}\text{\maroonD{one}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}}$.

choices.content: Five and seventy-one hundredths
Five thousand seventy-one
Five and seventy-one thousandths

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is $0.375$ in words?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $0.375$.

The last place value in the number is *thousandths*, so the number ends in the *thousandths* place.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $0$ |$.$  | $\greenD3$ | $\purpleC{7}$ | $\maroonD{5}$
The number $0$ is to the left of the decimal, so we do not need to write anything.

$.\greenD3\purpleC{7}\maroonD{5}$ is to the right of the decimal and ends in the *thousandths* place.

$\text{\greenD{Three hundred}}\text{\purpleC{ seventy-}}\text{\maroonD{five}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}}$
We write $0.375$ in words as $\text{\greenD{three hundred}}\text{\purpleC{ seventy-}}\text{\maroonD{five}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}}$.

choices.content: Three hundred seventy-five
Three hundred seventy-five hundredths
Three hundred seventy-five thousandths

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is $0.801$ in words?**

[[☃ radio 3]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $0.801$.

The last place value in the number is *thousandths*, so the number ends in the *thousandths* place.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $0$ |$.$  | $\greenD8$ | $\purpleC{0}$ | $\maroonD{1}$
The number $0$ is to the left of the decimal, so we do not need to write anything.

$.\greenD8\purpleC{0}\maroonD{1}$ is to the right of the decimal and ends in the *thousandths* place.

$\text{\greenD{Eight hundred}}\text{\maroonD{ one}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}}$
We write $0.801$ in words as $\text{\greenD{eight hundred}}\text{\maroonD{ one}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}}$.

choices.content: Eight hundred one
Eighty-one hundredths
Eight hundred one thousandths

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is $43.808$ in words?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $43.808$.

The last place value in the number is *thousandths*, so the number ends in the *thousandths* place.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\redD4$| $\goldD{3}$ |$.$  | $\greenD8$ | $\purpleC0$ | $\maroonD{8}$

The number $\redD4\goldD{3}$ is to the left of the decimal, so we can write it as $\text{\redD{forty-}\goldD{three}}$.

$\text{\redD{Forty-}\goldD{three}}-$

We can represent the decimal with the word $\blueD{\text{and}}$.

$\text{\redD{Forty-}\goldD{three}\blueD{ and}}-$

$.\greenD8\purpleC0\maroonD{8}$ is to the right of the decimal and ends in the *thousandths* place.

$\text{\greenD{eight hundred}}\text{\maroonD{ eight}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}}$.
We write $43.808$ in words as $\text{\redD{forty-}\goldD{three}\blueD{ and}}\text{\greenD{ eight hundred}}\text{\maroonD{ eight}}\text{\maroonD{ thousandths}} $

choices.content: Forty-three and eight hundred eight thousandths
Forty-three and eight hundred eight hundredths
Forty-three and eighty-eight thousandths

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is $630.5$ in words?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $630.5$.

The last place value in the number is *tenths*, so the number ends in the *tenths* place.

Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldD{6}$| $\greenD3$| $\purpleC0$ |$.$ | $\maroonD{5}$
The number $\goldD{6}\greenD3\purpleC0$ is to the left of the decimal, so we can write it as $\text{\goldD{six hundred}}\text{\greenD{ thirty}}$.

$\text{\goldD{Six hundred}}\text{\greenD{ thirty}}-$
We can represent the decimal with the word $\blueD{\text{and}}$.

$\text{\goldD{Six hundred}}\text{\greenD{ thirty}\blueD{ and}}-$
$.\maroonD{5}$ is to the right of the decimal and ends in the *tenths* place.

$\text{\maroonD{Five tenths}}$
We write $630.5$ in words as $\text{\goldD{six hundred}}\text{\greenD{ thirty}\blueD{ and }}\text{\maroonD{five tenths}}$.

choices.content: Six hundred three and five tenths
Six hundred thirty and five tenths
Six hundred thirty and five ones

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is $8{,}024.62$ in words?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $8{,}024.62$.

The last place value in the number is *hundredths*, so the number ends in the *hundredths* place.

Thousands| Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldD{8}$| $0$| $\greenD2$| $\redD4$ |$.$ | $\purpleC{6}$ | $\maroonD{2}$

The number $\goldD8{,}0\greenD2\redD4$ is to the left of the decimal, so we can write it as $\text{\goldD{eight thousand}}\text{\greenD{ twenty-}}\text{\redD{four}}$.

$\text{\goldD{Eight thousand}}\text{\greenD{ twenty-}}\text{\redD{four}}-$

We can represent the decimal with the word $\blueD{\text{and}}$.

$\text{\goldD{Eight thousand}}\text{\greenD{ twenty-}}\text{\redD{four}}\text{\blueD{ and}}-$

$.\purpleC{6}\maroonD{2}$ is to the right of the decimal and ends in the *hundredths* place.

$\text{\purpleC{Sixty-}}\text{\maroonD{two}}\text{\maroonD{ hundredths}}$

We write $8{,}024.62$ in words as $\text{\goldD{eight thousand}}\text{\greenD{ twenty-}}\text{\redD{four}}\text{\blueD{ and}} \text{\purpleC{ sixty-}}\text{\maroonD{two}}\text{\maroonD{ hundredths}}$.

choices.content: Eight hundred twenty-four and sixty-two tenths
Eight thousand twenty-four and sixty-two tenths
Eight thousand twenty-four and sixty-two hundredths

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is $70{,}110.9$ in words?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $70{,}110.9$.

The last place value in the number is *tenths*, so the number ends in the *tenths* place.

Ten Thousands | Thousands| Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths 
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldD{7}$| $0$| $\greenD1$| $\purpleC1$| $0$ |$.$ | $\maroonD{9}$

The number $\goldD70{,}\greenD1\purpleC10$ is to the left of the decimal, so we can write it as $\text{\goldD{seventy thousand}}\text{\greenD{ one hundred}}\text{\purpleC{ ten}}$.

$\text{\goldD{Seventy thousand}}\text{\greenD{ one hundred}}\text{\purpleC{ ten}}-$

We can represent the decimal with the word $\blueD{\text{and}}$.

$\text{\goldD{Seventy thousand}}\text{\greenD{ one hundred}}\text{\purpleC{ ten}}\text{\blueD{ and}}-$

$.\maroonD{9}$ is to the right of the decimal and ends in the *tenths* place.

$\text{\maroonD{Nine tenths}}$
We write $70{,}110.9$ in words as $\text{\goldD{seventy thousand}}\text{\greenD{ one hundred}}\text{\purpleC{ ten}}\text{\blueD{ and}} \text{\maroonD{ nine tenths}}$.

choices.content: Seventy thousand one hundred ten and nine tenths
Seven thousand one hundred ten and nine tenths
Seventy thousand one hundred and nine tenths

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the written form and decimal form that describe the same number.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to look at each written form one at a time.  

$\goldD{\text{Five}} {\text{ and }} \purpleC{\text{three hundredths}}$

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
| $\goldD{5}$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\purpleC{3}$ 

$\goldD{\text{Five}} {\text{ and }} \purpleC{\text{three hundredths}} = \goldD{5}.0\purpleC{3}$

$\goldD{\text{Five}} {\text{ and }} \greenD{\text{three tenths}}$

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: 
| $\goldD{5}$ |$.$  | $\greenD3$

$\goldD{\text{Five}} {\text{ and }} \greenD{\text{three tenths}} = \goldD{5}.\greenD{3}$

$\blueD{\text{Fifty-}} \goldD{\text{three}}$

Tens | Ones
:-:|:-: | :-: 
$\blueD{5}$ | $\goldD{3}$

$\blueD{\text{Fifty-}} \goldD{\text{three}} =\blueD{5}\goldD{3}$

The answer:

Written form | Decimal form
:-: | :-: 
Five and three hundredths | $5.03$
Five and three tenths | $5.3$
Fifty-three | $53$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the written form and decimal form that describe the same number.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to look at each written form one at a time.  

$\goldD{\text{Twenty-}}\purpleC{\text{two hundredths}}$

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
| $0$ |$.$  | $\goldD2$ | $\purpleC2$ 

$\goldD{\text{Twenty-}}\purpleC{\text{two hundredths}}= .\goldD{2}\purpleC{2}$

$\blueD{\text{Two Hundred}}$

Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: 
$\blueD{2}$ | $0$ | $0$

$\blueD{\text{Two Hundred}}=\blueD{2}00$

$\greenD{\text{Two thousandths}}$

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths | Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: 
$0$  |$.$ |$0$ | $0$ | $\greenD2$

$\greenD{\text{Two thousandths}} = 0.00\greenD{2}$

The answer:

Written form | Decimal form
:-: | :-: 
Two hundred | $200$
Twenty-two hundredths | $0.22$
Two thousandths | $0.002$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the written form and decimal form that describe the same number.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to look at each written form one at a time.  

$\goldD{\text{Nine}} {\text{ and }} \purpleC{\text{nine hundredths}}$

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
| $\goldD{9}$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\purpleC{9}$ 

$\goldD{\text{Nine}} {\text{ and }} \purpleC{\text{nine hundredths}} = \goldD{9}.0\purpleC{9}$

$\blueD{\text{Ninety}} {\text{ and }} \greenD{\text{nine tenths}}$

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: 
$\blueD{9}$| $0$ |$.$  | $\greenD9$

$\blueD{\text{Ninety}} {\text{ and }} \greenD{\text{nine tenths}} = \blueD{9}0.\greenD{9}$

$\greenD{\text{Ninety-}} \purpleC{\text{nine hundredths}}$

Tens | Ones | $.$| Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
 | $0$ |$.$| $\greenD{9}$ | $\purpleC{9}$

$\greenD{\text{Ninety-}} \purpleC{\text{nine hundredths}}=0.\greenD{9}\purpleC{9}$

The answer:

Written form | Decimal form
:-: | :-: 
Nine and nine hundredths | $9.09$
Ninety and nine tenths | $90.9$
Ninety-nine hundredths | $0.99$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the written form and decimal form that describe the same number.**

[[☃ matcher 2]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to look at each written form one at a time.  

$\blueD{\text{Forty-}} \goldD{\text{four}}$

Tens | Ones
:-:|:-: | :-: 
$\blueD{4}$ | $\goldD{4}$

$\blueD{\text{Forty-}} \goldD{\text{four}}=\blueD{4}\goldD{4}$

$\greenD{\text{Forty-}}\purpleC{\text{four thousandths}}$

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$0$| $.$ |$0$  | $\greenD{4}$ | $\purpleC{4}$ 

$\greenD{\text{Forty-}}\purpleC{\text{four thousandths}} = 0.0\greenD{4}\purpleC{4}$

$\maroonD{\text{Forty-}} \greenD{\text{four hundredths}}$

Tens | Ones | $.$| Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
 | $0$ |$.$| $\maroonD{4}$ | $\greenD{4}$

$\maroonD{\text{Forty-}} \greenD{\text{four hundredths}}=0.\maroonD{4}\greenD{4}$

The answer:

Written form | Decimal form
:-: | :-: 
Forty-four | $44$
Forty-four thousandths| $0.044$
Forty-four hundredths | $0.44$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the written form and decimal form that describe the same number.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to look at each written form one at a time.  

$\greenD{\text{Eighty-}}\purpleC{\text{seven thousandths}}$
